---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

class: slideblue

.size80[**Today's Agenda**]

<br>

.size50[.center[

???

]]

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2022)
]]

???

## Prep for Class
1. ...

CHECK OLD PUBLIC POLICY CLASS SLIDES ON TEMPORAL DISCOUNTING 14.2




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/05-1-Discounting.webp')
background-size: 90%
background-position: center

class: middle

???

Discounting is a hugely important concept in environmental problem-solving.

It simply means: To count at less than face value.

I'm hoping you covered this in some depth in Environmental Economics.

<br>

Last week we focused on risk.

HOW IS RISK A FORM OF DISCOUNTING SIMILAR TO WHAT YOU READ ABOUT FOR CLASS TODAY?

(Some people may be more or less risk acceptant BECAUSE they discount the future!)





---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

class: middle

???




---

class: middle, slideblue

.size60[**For Next Class**]

.size40[
1. 

2. 

3. 
]

???

QUESTIONS?
